test_cases.input: Please provide a website development proposal, including website architecture, database design, and user experience optimization.

arguments.system_design: A three-tier website architecture with a presentation layer (React.js frontend), an application layer (Node.js/Express backend), and a data layer (PostgreSQL). Incorporates CDN and load balancer for scalability and performance, along with secure API endpoints and modular components for maintainability.
arguments.technology_stack: Frontend: React.js, Backend: Node.js with Express, Database: PostgreSQL, Hosting: AWS EC2 & S3, CDN: CloudFront, Version Control: GitHub
arguments.hardware_requirements: Cloud-based hosting with minimum 2 vCPUs, 8GB RAM for app servers, AWS RDS for database, and 50GB SSD storage; scalable load balancer configuration for peak loads.
arguments.interaction_interface: Responsive web interface with mobile-first design, intuitive navigation, accessibility compliance (WCAG 2.1), and optimized load times; includes interactive UI elements and seamless database-driven content updates.
arguments.test_optimization_plan: Automated unit and integration testing using Jest and Cypress, load testing with Apache JMeter, continuous performance monitoring via New Relic, and iterative A/B testing for UI/UX refinement.

tools_called.name: system_architecture_designer

args.system_design: Modern responsive business/service company website with a public marketing site, service pages, lead capture forms, CMS-managed content, SEO-friendly routing, analytics, and optional authenticated client/admin areas for content and inquiry management. The architecture should prioritize scalability, maintainability, performance, accessibility, security, and ease of content updates.
args.technology_stack: Frontend: Next.js (React, TypeScript), Tailwind CSS or shadcn/ui, server-side rendering/static generation for SEO. Backend: Node.js with NestJS or Next.js API routes for lightweight use cases. Database: PostgreSQL with Prisma ORM. Cache/session layer: Redis. CMS: Headless CMS such as Strapi, Sanity, or Contentful. Hosting: Vercel for frontend, cloud managed Postgres (AWS RDS/Supabase/Neon), object storage for assets (S3-compatible). Analytics/monitoring: GA4, Tag Manager, Sentry, Log aggregation. CI/CD: GitHub Actions.
args.hardware_requirements: Typical cloud deployment; no dedicated on-prem hardware required. Production baseline: 2-4 vCPU, 4-8 GB RAM for backend services if self-hosted. Managed PostgreSQL with scalable storage and automated backups. CDN for static assets and global delivery. Redis instance for caching if needed. Sufficient storage for uploaded media, logs, and backups.
args.interaction_interface: Responsive web UI optimized for desktop, tablet, and mobile. Accessibility-compliant design with clear navigation, prominent calls to action, fast-loading pages, form validation feedback, and mobile-first layouts. Optional admin dashboard for content and lead management.
args.test_optimization_plan: Automated unit, integration, and end-to-end tests; performance testing for Core Web Vitals; accessibility testing with WCAG checks; SEO validation; security checks for forms and auth; staged deployments with preview environments; monitoring for uptime, errors, and conversion funnels. Use A/B testing on key CTA and landing pages, and iterate based on analytics and user feedback.
